steps.title: Identify the principle being illustrated
steps.content: The student notes that large-population countries can be less densely populated than small-population countries. The blank requires a comparison showing this: a country with a large population but low density, contrasted with a country having a smaller population but higher density.

steps.title: Match the data to the principle
steps.content:
Mali (17,600,000 inhabitants, 14.2 inhabitants/sq km density) paired with Lesotho (much lower population, higher density) perfectly demonstrates the principle—showing that more people does not mean higher density.

The other options either compare countries with similar densities, mix geographic area with density metrics, or track time-period population changes rather than illustrating the population-density relationship.

C is correct.
